name: Samsung Bot Handy
short_description: A concept home robot with a single AI-controlled arm and gripper that recognizes household objects and applies the right amount of force to handle them.
long_description: Unveiled by Samsung at CES 2021 and upgraded for CES 2022, Bot Handy is a wheeled mobile manipulator with one dexterous arm pitched as 'an extra hand' for chores like clearing tables, loading dishes, tidying rooms, and pouring drinks. It uses AI-based object recognition to distinguish objects by shape and material and to grasp delicate or transparent items without colliding. As a still-unreleased concept, it represents a vision of physical AI moving from cleaning and assistant appliances toward general-purpose manipulation in the home, where a robot must perceive a cluttered domestic scene and act on it physically.

images.caption: Samsung Bot Care (left) and Samsung Bot Handy (right), the single-arm mobile manipulator, as shown at CES 2021.
images.alt_text: A white wheeled robot with a single articulated arm and a two-finger gripper, standing beside a smaller cylindrical robot with a display screen.

sources.title: Samsung Introduces Latest Innovations for a Better Normal at CES 2021
sources.access_notes: Official CES 2021 newsroom announcement introducing Bot Handy as a concept robot.

sources.title: [CES 2022] Samsung Research's New Tech Trio - Samsung Bot Handy - Housework robot
sources.access_notes: Describes dexterous manipulation, general object handling, and extended workspace features.

interactions.title: Pour you a glass of wine
interactions.description: The single arm picks up a bottle, tilts it over a stemmed glass, and pours without overfilling or shattering the glass — the demo Samsung led with at CES 2021.

interactions.title: Clear the table into the dishwasher
interactions.description: It reads a cluttered table, decides a plate goes in the dishwasher rather than the fridge, and loads dishes one at a time — grasping each with the right force so nothing chips.

interactions.title: Arrange cut flowers in a vase
interactions.description: Beyond chores, the gripper picks up loose flowers and sets them into a vase, a delicate-handling demo Samsung used to show off material- and shape-aware grasping.

interactions.title: Stretch up to reach a high shelf
interactions.description: The body extends taller or shrinks shorter so the same arm can put away an item on an upper shelf or pick laundry off the floor, working across a three-dimensional room.

interactions.title: Grab the clear glass you can barely see
interactions.description: The CES 2022 upgrade lets it recognize transparent or partly hidden objects and pick up several dishes in a row without bumping the things around them.

interactions.title: Tidy a kid's room back to baseline
interactions.description: Roll through a strewn room and return loose toys, cups, and clothes to their bins and hampers — combining the wheeled base, vision, and per-object grasping the concept already pairs together.

interactions.title: Hand-off helper for someone with limited reach
interactions.description: Fetch a dropped item or bring a filled glass to a person who can't easily bend or stand, using the force-aware grip to pass fragile things gently — an assist grounded in its arm, mobility, and force sensing.
